6.1. Make available and support the required resources to drive asset management.
6.2. Where relevant, provide direction to and participate in reviews and oversight of asset management.

6.4. Review and approve this Policy prepared by, or on behalf of, the CISO.

<CLIENT>-IS-POL-00X

<!-- image -->

Abstract

This Policy defines the requirements for secure management of the assets owned and used by <CLIENT>, throughout its lifecycle.

**STATEMENT OF CONFIDENTIALITY**

All information in this document is <CLIENT> PRIVILEGED and for INTERNAL USE ONLY.

The information in this document may contain <CLIENT> proprietary trade secrets and confidential information, the disclosure of which could substantially benefit competitors. As a res

<CLIENT> shall: 
            5.8.1 Ensure that usage of all organization’s information, information assets and information and communications technology (ICT) systems are approved by the information security department prior to deployment.
            5.8.2 Establish, document, and circulate the rules for the acceptable use of assets in line with the <CLIENT> Acceptable Use Policy (<CLIENT>-ORG-IMS-POL-002) and disciplinary procedures.
            5.8.3 Consider the information provided by other

<CLIENT> shall: 
            5.9.1 Provide means to securely access organizational information and information assets via ‘Bring Your Own Device’ (BYOD). 
            5.9.2 Establish and implement information security requirements to secure organizational information and information assets via ‘Bring Your Own Device’ (BYOD) in line with <CLIENT> Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) Policy (<CLIENT>-ORG-IMS-POL-020).

3 SCOPE

This Policy applies to all employees and critical third parties who access or manage information and information assets or use the organization’s information and communications technology (ICT) systems.

**Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure’.**
3 SCOPE

This Policy applies to all employees and critical third parties who access or manage information and information assets or use the organizati

5.1 Asset Inventory

<CLIENT>; shall:
5.1.1. Identify assets relevant in the information lifecycle (creation, processing, storage, transmission, deletion, destruction, and protection) and document their importance.
5.1.2. Ensure that all organizational assets are considered for the asset management process using a centralized and efficient approach.
5.1.3. Ensure that documentation is done in dedicated asset inventories as appropriate. The asset inventory at a minimum shall contain:
5.1.3.1. Typ

<CLIENT> shall:
            5.2.1 Establish and implement a process to ensure timely assignment of asset ownership.
            5.2.2 Assign/reassign ownership when assets are created or transferred.
            5.2.3 Assign ownership to individuals having approved management responsibility across the asset lifecycle. Asset owners shall have the following responsibilities at a minimum:
                a) Perform inventory of the applicable assets.
                b) Classify and protect the asse

Executives & Senior Management shall:
6.1. Make available and support the required resources to drive asset management.
6.2. Where relevant, provide direction to and participate in reviews and oversight of asset management.
6.3. IMS Committee to:
6.4. Review and approve this Policy prepared by, or on behalf of, the CISO.
6.5. Establish process for the assignment of asset ownership and its periodical review.
6.6. Assign an owner with management responsibility for each identified asset across asse

<CLIENT> shall: 
            5.8.1 Ensure that usage of all organization’s information, information assets and information and communications technology (ICT) systems are approved by the information security department prior to deployment.
            5.8.2 Establish, document, and circulate the rules for the acceptable use of assets in line with the <CLIENT> Acceptable Use Policy (<CLIENT>-ORG-IMS-POL-002) and disciplinary procedures.
            5.8.3 Consider the information provided by other

<CLIENT> shall:
            5.4.1 Develop procedures for handling, processing, storing, and communicating information consistent with its classification level. The following items should be considered, at a minimum:
            5.4.2 Access restrictions supporting the protection requirements for each level of classification.
            5.4.3 Protection of temporary or permanent copies of information to a level consistent with the protection of the original information.
            5.4.4 Storage

<CLIENT> shall:
            5.3.1 Label information assets in physical and electronic formats according to the established information classification scheme as per the <CLIENT> Data Security Policy (<CLIENT>-ORG-IMS-POL-003).
                a) Develop detailed procedures to give guidance on where and how labels are attached in consideration of how the information is accessed or the assets are handled depending on the types of media.
                b) Ensure that output from systems carry an ap

<CLIENT> shall: 
            5.9.1 Provide means to securely access organizational information and information assets via ‘Bring Your Own Device’ (BYOD). 
            5.9.2 Establish and implement information security requirements to secure organizational information and information assets via ‘Bring Your Own Device’ (BYOD) in line with <CLIENT> Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) Policy (<CLIENT>-ORG-IMS-POL-020).

3 SCOPE

This Policy applies to all employees and critical third parties who access or manage information and information assets or use the organization’s information and communications technology (ICT) systems.

**Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure’.**
